title: MANGA NGE Is it just me
display_title: MANGA NGE Is it just me
wikitext:
[]: [NGE] Is it just me?             Quote Post by Evangelion Oi! » Fri Nov 03, 2006 12:49 am is it just me? or do the characters seem to be looking older as the volumes of eva come take a look at volume 3 Rei and Shinji and compare them to volume 9 Rei and Shinji big differnece Rei looks like shes at least 17 and Shinji like 16 or something i dont know if sadamoto did it on purpose or on accident but it look cool

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Nov 03, 2006 1:59 pm I see what you're talking about, but it's probably not intentional. Sadamoto has been doing the manga for about 12 years now, and you can understand how his drawing may have changed in that time. Personally, I'm impressed by his consistancy. He's improved, but he's managed to do it in such a way that it's not so far flung from the original. Amusing examples include Megatokyo and Penny Arcade . If you compare early strips to current strips, they hardly look like they're from the same comic. Granted, Mike and Fred started from little artistic skill, unlike Sadamoto, but you see a lot of comic series progess in such a way that the comic goes through such change that at some point it ceases to be the same thing, at least in terms of its visuals. I'm glad that the Eva manga hasn't gone through that.

Article: MANGA NGE Is it just me
[]: [NGE] Is it just me?             Quote Post by Evangelion Oi! » Fri Nov 03, 2006 12:49 am is it just me? or do the characters seem to be looking older as the volumes of eva come take a look at volume 3 Rei and Shinji and compare them to volume 9 Rei and Shinji big differnece Rei looks like shes at least 17 and Shinji like 16 or something i dont know if sadamoto did it on purpose or on accident but it look cool

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Nov 03, 2006 1:59 pm I see what you're talking about, but it's probably not intentional. Sadamoto has been doing the manga for about 12 years now, and you can understand how his drawing may have changed in that time. Personally, I'm impressed by his consistancy. He's improved, but he's managed to do it in such a way that it's not so far flung from the original. Amusing examples include Megatokyo and Penny Arcade . If you compare early strips to current strips, they hardly look like they're from the same comic. Granted, Mike and Fred started from little artistic skill, unlike Sadamoto, but you see a lot of comic series progess in such a way that the comic goes through such change that at some point it ceases to be the same thing, at least in terms of its visuals. I'm glad that the Eva manga hasn't gone through that.
